# Volere Requirements Specification Template - Cite-Only Pointer

This document is a cite-only pointer. It names the Volere Requirements Specification Template and the concepts the BRD Builder workflow references, links to the upstream publisher, and does not redistribute the template text or any prose definitions from it.

## What Volere Is

Volere is a long-standing requirements-engineering template authored by James and Suzanne Robertson (Atlantic Systems Guild). It provides a sectioned shell for a requirements specification and a per-requirement "requirement shell" form covering attributes such as identifier, type, description, rationale, source, fit criterion, customer satisfaction and dissatisfaction, priority, conflicts, supporting materials, and history.

The BRD Builder references Volere by name in two ways:

* As the most commonly cited shell when business analysts ask "what sections should a BRD have?". The BRD Builder ships its own canonical `HVE-Core BRD v1.0` template instead of redistributing Volere's shell.
* As the source of the per-requirement attribute set that informed the canonical requirement-statement form documented in [../SKILL.md#canonical-statement-form](../SKILL.md#canonical-statement-form). The BRD Builder's form is original Microsoft content; it does not reproduce Volere's prose.

## Why Cite-Only

The Volere template is published under a license that grants in-organization use but does not permit redistribution in an open-source agent collection. Embedding the template text, the requirement-shell labels, or Volere's definition prose is therefore out of scope for this repository.

The BRD Builder's posture is:

* Reference Volere by name when explaining the BRD Builder's section structure or per-requirement attribute set to a stakeholder.
* Do not paraphrase Volere's prose. Definitions in this repository are written from scratch as original Microsoft content.
* Do not embed the Volere template shell. The canonical template the BRD Builder uses is [`brd-author/templates/brd-full.md`](../../brd-author/templates/brd-full.md), authored under Microsoft CC BY 4.0.

## Upstream Source

[https://www.volere.org/](https://www.volere.org/) - publisher's site, where the template, its license terms, and current revision are obtained.
